title: getting better with the matty flips 👀 #fpv #drone #freestyle
creator: JML Films
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one new exemplar diversifying this pack's short-form FPV-freestyle-trick subgenre (distinct from this pack's existing scenic/cinematic FPV shorts, e.g. flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's single continuous forest-canopy pass, or drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's waterfall dive -- this record documents trick-focused freestyle flying, specifically repeated attempts at the 'Matty Flip' FPV maneuver). Verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/shgIvccHfcE&format=json), which returned title='getting better with the matty flips 👀 #fpv #drone #freestyle' (exact field-for-field match to the candidate brief's title), provider_name='YouTube', thumbnail_url='https://i.ytimg.com/vi/shgIvccHfcE/hq2.jpg', and embed dimensions height=200/width=113 (a portrait/vertical aspect ratio, consistent with native Shorts format). IMPORTANT DISCREPANCY FOUND AND CORRECTED: the discovery-stage candidate brief supplied for this task claimed creator='MattyFleisch (Matt Fleischer)', but this session's own oEmbed verification returned author_name='JML Films' and author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@JML_Films' for this exact source_url -- i.e. the channel that actually published this specific video is 'JML Films', not Matt Fleischer's own channel. A supplementary WebSearch this session ('Matt Fleischer' OR 'MattyFleisch' fpv drone freestyle 'matty flip') independently confirmed that a real FPV pilot named Matt Fleischer ('MattyFleisch') exists, runs the website mattyfleisch.com and a YouTube channel ('Mattyfleisch FPV', https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCI5r6uGXPRQ4J57sPmkcLtg) and Instagram (@mattyfleischfpv) -- so 'Matty Flip' as a named FPV trick is plausibly associated with or named after this real individual (one search snippet also associated the trick name with a separate pilot, 'MattyStuntz', so even the trick's namesake is not fully disambiguated in this session's research) -- but none of this establishes that Matt Fleischer personally piloted, filmed, or published THIS specific upload. Per this task's constraint against fabricating a creator claim, and per this repo's grounding rules ('never confirm a user's assumption ... without independently verifying it'), the discovery-stage creator claim is NOT adopted here; `creator` is instead set to the directly-verified oEmbed author_name 'JML Films'. A WebFetch of the Shorts page itself (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/shgIvccHfcE) and of the channel page (https://www.youtube.com/@JML_Films) both returned only YouTube's generic truncated footer/navigation/copyright boilerplate (JS-rendered content not extractable via this session's fetch tooling) -- no description text, upload date, view/like counts, subscriber count, or Creative Commons marking was recoverable from either fetch; this is disclosed rather than papered over, matching this pack's existing precedent for JS-rendered YouTube pages (e.g. flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json, rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json). A further WebSearch this session ('"JML Films" youtube fpv drone freestyle channel') found no independent corroboration of this channel's size, reach, or reputation -- it did not appear among prominent FPV-freestyle channel results, consistent with this being a small/personal channel rather than an established professional media brand. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no explicit Creative Commons marking was found in either the oEmbed response (which carries no license field) or the two WebFetch attempts, per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `platform` is 'youtube', confirmed both by the oEmbed provider_name field and the source_url's own host (youtube.com/shorts/...); this is not an Instagram URL, so the Instagram media-extraction prohibition is not triggered (is_instagram=false, independently re-confirmed this session, not merely re-stated from discovery). `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not carry a duration field for this URL, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session; rather than fabricate a plausible-looking runtime, this field stays unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual FPV creator's hashtag-driven Shorts upload documenting trick practice, with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint, and no live playback of this specific video was performed in this session. Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.70 and exposure=0.70 sit at the low end of this pack's documented 0.68-0.88 band, slightly below flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json's 0.72/0.72 (that record's baseline reflects a scenic, cinematic single-take FPV pass with no positive OR negative disclosed signal) -- here there is likewise no disclosed drone/camera model, resolution, or export setting anywhere in the title, hashtags (#fpv #drone #freestyle -- generic, no model-specific tag), or the (truncated) channel page, AND the video's own title contains a genuine, disclosed negative-leaning signal absent from the flaviufpv comparison: the phrase 'getting better with the matty flips' is the uploader's own self-description of a maneuver still being refined/practiced, i.e. a progression/practice clip rather than a fully polished showcase take -- a real, text-grounded reason to calibrate marginally below a neutral-baseline scenic FPV short. motion_smoothness=0.72 is set marginally higher than sharpness/exposure (0.70), following this pack's established convention of crediting motion quality when trick-execution itself is the clip's subject (see rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json's and acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json's identical reasoning for single-continuous-move genres): performing a 'Matty Flip' -- a named, recognized FPV acrobatic maneuver -- at all, even one still being refined, requires and demonstrates a real baseline of throttle/attitude control beyond a beginner clip, though this remains a qualitative genre-based judgment, not a frame-level motion measurement, and is capped below flaviufpv's 0.74 by the same 'still improving' self-disclosure that lowered sharpness/exposure. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-estimated: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.70, exposure=0.70, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights(weights_version='default-v1')` (equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.7066666666666666 (rounded to 0.7067 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in two structural facts obtained via this session's own YouTube oEmbed fetch, not a guess from the title alone: (1) the source_url itself is a /shorts/ path -- YouTube's own platform-level designation for its short-form vertical product; (2) the oEmbed JSON response's embed dimensions (height=200, width=113) describe a portrait/vertical aspect ratio, consistent with vertical short-form content and inconsistent with the 16:9 landscape aspect ratio of this pack's long-form cinematic exemplars. The title's own hashtag-driven, trick-focused framing ('getting better with the matty flips 👀 #fpv #drone #freestyle') further matches this pack's short-form-reel genre definition (fast-paced, hashtag-driven, sub-60-second format). No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session: WebFetch of both the Shorts page and the channel page returned only generic JS-rendered footer/navigation boilerplate, with no description text, cut information, or shot-length data extractable; a WebSearch for the channel and trick name likewise surfaced no creator commentary, interview, or press coverage describing this specific video's cut count, shot length, or transition style. estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed therefore stay null/empty rather than guessed -- the title's plural 'flips' phrasing could plausibly imply multiple attempts/cuts stitched together, but this is a linguistic inference, not a confirmed structural fact, so it is deliberately NOT converted into a numeric cut-count guess.
